care.summary: A rare and sought-after philodendron with dark green leaves splashed with vivid pink variegation.
care.light: Bright indirect light is essential for pink variegation
care.water: Water when top inch of soil dries; avoid overwatering
care.soil: Well-draining aroid mix with orchid bark, perlite, and peat
care.humidity: Moderate to high; 50-70% ideal for large leaf growth
care.fertilizer: Balanced liquid fertilizer monthly spring through summer
care.toxicity: Toxic if ingested; keep away from pets and children
common_issues: Reverting to all-green leaves in low light
Small leaves from insufficient humidity or nutrients
Root rot in compacted, water-retentive soil
Brown leaf tips from low humidity
tips: Provide a moss pole for climbing — leaves grow 2-3x larger
Prune green-only stems to encourage pink growth
High humidity is key for pink variegation emergence
Propagate from stem cuttings with at least one node
